author: y4rr
content:
Landed tensor-level p2p + DeviceMesh; DTensor split out to #611.

## What landed

### \`ferrotorch-distributed/src/p2p.rs\`
Tensor-level wrappers around the existing byte-level \`Backend::send\` / \`Backend::recv\`:
- \`send(tensor, dst_rank, backend)\` — typed payload, dimension-checked
- \`recv(shape, src_rank, backend) -> Tensor\` — allocates a fresh tensor of the requested shape
- \`recv_with_timeout\`, \`recv_into\`, \`recv_into_with_timeout\` — variants for caller-owned buffers and configurable timeouts
- \`sendrecv(send_tensor, recv_shape, peer, backend)\` — atomic two-party exchange with rank-ordered ordering (lower rank sends first then receives, higher rank receives first then sends). Avoids the deadlock-avoidance dance callers would otherwise have to write themselves.

All shape / dtype / rank validity checks happen at call time so misuse fails before any byte hits the wire.

### \`ferrotorch-distributed/src/device_mesh.rs\`
\`DeviceMesh\` is an n-D arrangement of ranks (e.g. 2-D \`[dp, tp]\`, 3-D \`[dp, tp, pp]\`). Public surface:
- \`new(shape, world_size)\` — verifies \`prod(shape) == world_size\`
- \`new_with_names(shape, names, world_size)\` — attach names to dims (\`["dp", "tp"]\`)
- \`coords(rank) -> Vec<usize>\` — row-major coordinate within the mesh
- \`rank_of(coords) -> usize\` — inverse
- \`ranks_along_dim(dim, rank)\` — ranks that match \`rank\` on every other dim (the per-axis collective group containing \`rank\`)
- \`groups_along_dim(dim)\` — partitioning of the world into all per-axis groups; useful for bulk sub-backend creation
- \`dim_index(name)\` — resolve a dim name to its index

The mesh is infrastructure-agnostic: it does index math only. Sub-group construction is left to the existing \`SubBackend\` (which can take the rank list from \`groups_along_dim\`).

### \`all_to_all\`
The collective was already implemented in \`collective.rs\` (\`all_to_all\`, \`all_to_all_with_timeout\`, \`all_to_all_single_uneven\`) — verified working alongside the new p2p ops.

## Tests (15 new)
\`p2p::tests\` (5):
- send/recv roundtrip across two simulated backends
- recv_into overwrites in place
- send rejects self-rank
- recv rejects out-of-bounds rank
- sendrecv swaps two peers (asymmetric exchange)

\`device_mesh::tests\` (10):
- shape-product mismatch and zero-dim rejected
- 2-D coord roundtrip on \`[2, 4]\`
- ranks_along_dim returns correct axis (rank 5 in [2, 4]: dim 0 → [1, 5], dim 1 → [4..=7])
- groups_along_dim partitions the world
- dim names resolve to indices
- 3-D mesh on \`[2, 2, 3]\` (rank 7 → coords (1, 0, 1))
- bounds checks on rank and coord inputs

## Verification
- \`cargo test -p ferrotorch-distributed --lib p2p::\`: 5/5
- \`cargo test -p ferrotorch-distributed --lib device_mesh::\`: 10/10
- \`cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast --features cuda\`: zero regressions

## Internal change
Made \`floats_to_bytes\` and \`bytes_to_floats\` in \`collective.rs\` \`pub(crate)\` so the new \`p2p\` module can share them without duplicating the unsafe reinterpret logic.

## Out of scope → #611
Full DTensor abstraction (sharded tensor over a DeviceMesh, with placement specs and automatic collective insertion on placement transitions). It's a ~3K-LOC surface that ties autograd + collectives + mesh — better landed as a focused issue.
